findings.evidence: Lines 11-12 are `public import ...FiniteAdeleRing` and `public import ...SelmerGroup`; the only in-file uses found by grep are proof/body support such as `HeightOneSpectrum.Support.finite` at lines 244/246 and `valuationOfNeZero` at lines 77/101, while the exported characteristic statements use the valuation/log/count API directly at lines 98-99, 260-271, and 278-281.
findings.fix: Make these proof-support imports ordinary `import`s, or move the needed private proof support behind a non-reexported helper, keeping only the modules whose names occur in the exported divisor statements as `public import`.
findings.issue: `Dedekind.lean` publicly reexports `Mathlib.RingTheory.DedekindDomain.FiniteAdeleRing` and `SelmerGroup`, exposing finite adeles and Selmer-group API to every downstream user importing the divisor order system.

summary: The Dedekind divisor API has the expected coefficient/support/effectivity lemmas, but the module reexports unrelated Mathlib APIs as part of this public surface.
